description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, roughly triangular stone fragment with a weathered, tan-brown surface. The front face features faded reddish-brown markings that resemble ancient or primitive pictographs, possibly handprints or symbolic figures. The stone's edges are jagged and uneven, with one side showing a rough, broken texture. The reverse side has a white label with blue handwritten text reading 'JC679', suggesting cataloging or archival use. The stone appears dense and solid, with a granular, natural stone texture visible on all sides. Its overall appearance suggests it may have been part of a larger structure or artifact, now isolated as a single piece.
description: A jagged, tan-brown stone fragment with faded reddish pictographs on one face and a catalog label on the reverse.
description_view_0: The front face displays faded reddish-brown pictographs on a tan, weathered stone surface.
description_view_1: A side view highlighting the stone's thin, irregular profile and rough, broken edge.
description_view_2: The reverse side shows a white label with blue handwritten text 'JC679' on the stone's coarse surface.
description_view_3: Another angled rear view, emphasizing the label and the stone's jagged, granular texture.
attribute_location: archaeological site or urban park exhibit
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or displayed on a plinth
attribute_pattern: Faded reddish pictographs resembling handprints or figures, irregular and non-repetitive, with no geometric regularity.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible surface erosion and edge chipping, indicating prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with man-made structures or display settings, often isolated for visibility and preservation.
